 {"id":"ft-ytyo","title":"[EPIC] Phase 3: Full agent support — complete packs + account rotation","description":"# Phase 3 — Full Agent Support

## Objective
Expand from “works for the main path” to “works broadly”:
- Complete patterns and workflows for **Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini**.
- Harden account rotation logic and integrate usage tracking as a first-class system.

## Why this phase exists
Agent CLIs change output formats over time; partial coverage creates fragility. This phase invests in:
- more complete detection packs
- richer structured extraction
- regression fixtures (“golden corpus”)
- workflow variants per agent

## Deliverables
- Rule packs:
  - `core.codex`: full coverage for usage warnings/reached/session summaries/auth prompts.
  - `core.claude_code`: compaction + banner + usage signals (as discovered in field).
  - `core.gemini`: usage reached + session summary + model changes + resume mechanics.

- Workflows:
  - `handle_usage_limits` implemented for all three agents (behavior may differ).
  - `handle_session_end` (capture + store structured session summaries).
  - `handle_auth_required` (centralize login flows).

- Accounts:
  - `caut` becomes the source of truth for usage; wa stores a historical mirror.
  - Selection policy (percent remaining + LRU tie-break).

## Acceptance criteria
- Primary detections for all three agents are covered by unit tests and golden corpus fixtures.
- At least one real-world end-to-end run exists for each agent’s usage-limit handling path (or documented safe fallback if truly impossible to automate).



## Success Criteria
- Rule packs and workflows expand to support all target agents (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini) with consistent behavior.
- Account rotation and agent-specific workflows are covered by regression tests and golden corpora.
- User-facing surfaces (robot/MCP/human CLI) remain stable as capabilities expand.


## Testing
- Golden corpus coverage:
  - Every new rule added in this phase must land with fixtures (positive + near-miss negative).
  - Corpus tests must fail loudly on drift and point to the exact rule + capture that changed.

- Cross-agent workflow regression tests:
  - Use shared workflow engine tests plus per-agent fixtures to validate semantics remain consistent (idempotency, resume, safe failure).

- E2E per-agent reality checks:
  - For each agent (Codex/Claude/Gemini), capture at least one real transcript and ensure it’s represented in fixtures.
  - If true automation is not possible (MFA), require an E2E “fails safe” scenario that still produces artifacts and instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

- `docs_gen.rs` / `wa-robot-envelope.json`: well-known robot error-code docs are materially narrower than the actual emitted `robot.*` surface.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:52:52.271556Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-17T01:07:25.759821Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","contracts","review"],"comments":[{"id":2370,"issue_id":"ft-yv9rh","author":"MagentaWillow","text":"Progress: fixed public robot envelope/docs drift so docs/json-schema/wa-robot-envelope.json and docs_gen.rs now document live core codes emitted by main.rs, including robot.approval_error, robot.require_approval, robot.rate_limited, robot.fts_query_error, and specific robot.wezterm_* backend failures.  {"id":"ft-yy4a","title":"FTUI-08.2 Optimize hot paths identified by baseline deltas","description":"## Background\nAny regressions found in T81 must be resolved systematically.\n\n## Deliverables\n- targeted optimization tasks with proofs\n- regression tests/benchmarks for fixed hotspots\n- documented tradeoffs for each optimization\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- key regressions are eliminated or accepted with rationale\n- optimizations preserve correctness invariants.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:09.967458751Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.217360Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T10:08:00.684261226Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-yy4a","depends_on_id":"ft-rs2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-yy4a","depends_on_id":"ft-xohn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-yy9z","title":"[Human command] wa stop (stop watcher in workspace safely)","description":"# Task: Human command wa stop

## Goal
Give users a safe, ergonomic way to stop a running `wa watch` for a given workspace.

Many users will run `wa watch` in the background (or via terminal multiplexers). Telling them to manually find and kill a PID is user-hostile.

## Behavior (v0)
- `wa stop`
  - resolves the workspace (flag/env/default)
  - reads the watch lock metadata (pid/started_at/host/version)
  - sends SIGTERM to the watcher pid (preferred) and waits for the lock to be released
  - prints a clear summary:
    - which workspace
    - which pid
    - whether shutdown was graceful

- `wa stop --force`
  - if graceful shutdown times out, escalates to SIGKILL (only with explicit `--force`)

## Safety
- Never kills an arbitrary PID:
  - only act if the lock metadata matches the workspace lock
  - require the process to be owned by the same user (best-effort check)

## UX
- TTY: rich panel output.
- Non-TTY: stable JSON.

## Testing
- Integration test:
  - start a watcher-like process that holds the lock
  - run `wa stop`
  - assert lock is released and process exits

- E2E:
  - `wa-4vx.10.21` validates stop+restart behavior and captures artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa stop` reliably stops a watcher started in the same workspace.
- `wa stop` errors are actionable when no watcher is running.

 {"id":"ft-z0e","title":"[EPIC] Session Recording and Replay: capture, store, and replay agent sessions","description":"# [EPIC] Session Recording and Replay

## Mission
Enable users to record complete terminal sessions, replay them for analysis/debugging, and share them for collaboration/documentation.

## Why This Matters
Current state: wa captures segments and events, but users cannot:
- See a full session from start to finish
- Replay what happened to understand issues
- Share a recording with others
- Use recordings for demos or documentation

Session recording solves all these:
- **Debugging**: "What exactly happened before the crash?"
- **Collaboration**: "Here's a recording of the issue"
- **Documentation**: "Watch this recording to learn the workflow"
- **Compliance**: "Full audit trail of agent actions"

## Core Insight: Time-Travel Debugging
Recordings are like git for terminal sessions—you can move backward and forward through time, seeing exactly what happened at each moment.

## Scope

### Recording
- Start/stop recording for specific panes
- Automatic recording based on rules (e.g., "record all Codex panes")
- Metadata capture (timestamps, pane info, agent type)
- Efficient storage (delta-compressed)

### Replay
- Terminal-accurate playback at configurable speed
- Pause, seek, speed control
- Event annotations shown during replay
- Workflow step markers

### Export
- Self-contained recording files (.war = wa recording)
- Shareable without wa installed (HTML player option)
- Privacy controls (redact sensitive content)

### Integration
- `wa record start --pane 0`
- `wa record stop`
- `wa replay session-123.war`
- `wa replay --speed 2x`
- TUI replay viewer

## Success Criteria
- Recording adds <5% overhead to capture
- Replay is terminal-accurate (escape sequences preserved)
- Recordings can be shared and played back
- Users report recordings help with debugging

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for delta compression
- Integration tests for record/replay roundtrip
- E2E tests for full workflow recording
- Fuzz tests for malformed recording files

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Recording start/stop commands functional
- [ ] Recordings stored efficiently (delta-compressed)
- [ ] Replay command plays back accurately
- [ ] Speed control works (0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x)
- [ ] Event annotations visible during replay
- [ ] Export to shareable format works
- [ ] Privacy redaction configurable
- [ ] Tests cover all functionality","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:48:22.423132786Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:45.975377588Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:45.975311194Z","close_reason":"All child beads completed; closing stale-open epic to keep active plan set non-duplicative.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
